data.title: EPAM Awards Spotlight AI And Cloud Ambitions After Share Price Slide
data.description:
• EPAM Systems (NYSE:EPAM) has been named a 2026 Google Cloud Partner of the Year in two categories.
  • The awards recognize EPAM's work in AI driven solutions, sustainability, and complex cloud migrations.
  • This recognition highlights EPAM's role in delivering measurable business outcomes for enterprise clients on Google Cloud.

For investors following EPAM Systems at a current share price of $118.98, this new recognition arrives after an extended period of weak share performance, including a 40.6% decline year to date and a 57.9% decline over three years. The awards put a fresh spotlight on EPAM's capabilities in AI, sustainability, and large scale cloud projects, which are central to many enterprise technology budgets.

The Google Cloud awards may help reinforce EPAM's position with large clients that are already committing significant spend to AI and cloud modernization. Investors can watch how often this recognition is referenced in new deal announcements, client wins, or partnership expansions, as that would help show how much commercial traction EPAM is getting from this increased visibility.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for EPAM Systems by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on EPAM Systems.

Does the team leading EPAM Systems have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

The Google Cloud Partner of the Year awards tie directly to EPAM’s executive agenda, because they validate how leadership is positioning the company around AI, sustainability, and complex cloud work rather than traditional staff augmentation. The recognition for sustainability solutions and an enterprise database migration for a European financial institution points to EPAM’s ability to win large, long-cycle projects against global players such as Accenture, Cognizant, and Infosys. For you as an investor, this matters less as a marketing headline and more as a real world check on whether management’s focus areas are resonating with big budget clients at a time when the share price has been under pressure. The awards also suggest that EPAM’s leadership team is executing on its push into higher value consulting and end to end transformation projects, which ties into prior commentary about returns on capital and earnings growth. The key question is whether this kind of external validation translates into a steadier pipeline and better utilization as management works through sector wide scrutiny of IT services spending.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The EPAM Systems Narrative

  • The recognition for AI and cloud solutions lines up with the narrative that EPAM is moving up the value chain by focusing on complex, ROI driven transformation work rather than lower margin custom development.
  • These awards raise the bar for execution. If EPAM cannot keep winning similar large scale projects while competitors like Accenture and Tata Consultancy Services push hard in AI services, that would challenge the growth story.
  • The sustainability award, tied to geospatial and AI capabilities, introduces an environmental solutions angle that is not fully reflected in the broader narrative about AI driven transformation and margin expansion.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for EPAM Systems to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Awards do not guarantee future project wins, so there is a risk that expectations for AI and cloud led growth get ahead of actual contract volume and pricing.
  • ⚠️ As hyperscalers like Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and AWS expand their own services arms, EPAM could face tighter competition for the kind of projects that underpinned these awards.
  • 🎁 Third party recognition for complex AI and database work supports the view that EPAM’s technical depth can help it secure larger, more critical roles within enterprise digital programs.
  • 🎁 The sustainability focused award highlights an additional use case for EPAM’s AI and data skills, potentially widening the opportunity set across regulated and ESG oriented clients.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the useful indicators are not the awards themselves but the follow through. Watch for management commentary on how often these Google Cloud wins help EPAM get invited to larger requests for proposals, whether the company references new AI and sustainability engagements in earnings calls, and how concentrated these projects are by client or region. Given the recent share price weakness, many investors will be looking for signs that leadership can convert technical recognition into steadier bookings, a healthier mix of high margin work, and a more durable position alongside larger IT services peers.

data.title: Is Rolls-Royce (LSE:RR.) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Rolls-Royce Holdings is reasonably priced or getting ahead of itself, the current share price is only the starting point of the story.
  • The stock closed at £11.29, with a 13.8% decline over the last 7 days and a 5.4% decline over 30 days, yet it still shows a 50.8% return over 1 year and a very large gain over 5 years that is close to 10x.
  • Investors have been reacting to ongoing updates around the business and its sector, which continue to shape expectations about future prospects and risk. These shifts in sentiment help explain why the share price has pulled back recently even after very strong multi year returns.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Rolls-Royce Holdings currently scores 4 out of 6. Next up is a closer look at the different valuation methods behind that score and, later in the article, an even more complete way to think about what the stock might be worth.

Rolls-Royce Holdings delivered 50.8% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Aerospace & Defense industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Rolls-Royce Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return.

For Rolls-Royce Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about £3.6b. Analysts provide cash flow estimates for several years ahead, and Simply Wall St then extends these out further. By 2030, the projected free cash flow used in the model is about £4.3b, with interim annual projections between 2026 and 2035 based on a mix of analyst sources and extrapolated estimates.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of £9.20 per share. Compared with the current share price of £11.29, this implies the stock is about 22.8% overvalued according to this DCF view.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Rolls-Royce Holdings may be overvalued by 22.8%. Discover 8 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Rolls-Royce Holdings.

Approach 2: Rolls-Royce Holdings Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Rolls-Royce Holdings, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is generating. A higher or lower P/E often reflects what investors expect for future growth and how much risk they see in those earnings. Strong growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower one.

Rolls-Royce Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 16.0x. That sits below the peer average of 23.1x and well below the Aerospace & Defense industry average of 51.2x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for the P/E, which for Rolls-Royce Holdings is 23.9x. This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it factors in company specific elements such as earnings growth, profit margins, risk profile, market cap and industry context.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 23.9x with the current P/E of 16.0x suggests the shares are trading below the level implied by these fundamentals.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Meet Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St's Community page that lets you attach your own story about Rolls-Royce Holdings to the numbers by linking a view on its future revenue, earnings and margins to a forecast and a Fair Value, then comparing that Fair Value with the current price to help you decide whether the stock looks attractive or stretched.

Each Narrative is updated when new information arrives, such as earnings or news, so your Fair Value view stays aligned with what is happening, and you can easily see how your stance compares with others on the platform used by millions of investors.

For Rolls-Royce Holdings, one investor might align with the more optimistic narrative that supports a Fair Value of £17.40 based on higher growth and margins, while another might lean toward a more cautious view closer to £5.78 that focuses on execution and structural risks. Narratives lets you place yourself clearly on that spectrum and see how your assumptions translate into a price you are comfortable with.

For Rolls-Royce Holdings, however, we’ll make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Rolls-Royce Holdings Narratives:

Each one ties concrete assumptions on growth, margins and valuation to a Fair Value, so you can see how different views on the same company can lead to very different conclusions about today’s share price.

🐂 Rolls-Royce Holdings Bull Case

Fair Value: £14.27

Implied undervaluation vs last close: around 21%.

Assumed revenue growth: 8.8% a year.

  • Analysts in this camp work with assumptions that revenue grows and margins moderate from current levels, which in turn are used to derive earnings of about £4.2b by 2029 if Rolls-Royce delivers on guidance.
  • This view focuses on robust aftermarket activity in Civil Aerospace and Defence, continued cash generation, and the potential support of buybacks and improving balance sheet strength.
  • Clean energy and next generation projects, including SMRs and UltraFan, are treated as potentially meaningful long term contributors, while the narrative also flags execution, regulation and capital intensity as important watchpoints.

🐻 Rolls-Royce Holdings Bear Case

Fair Value: £8.36

Implied overvaluation vs last close: around 26%.

Assumed revenue growth: 3.5% a year.

  • The bearish cohort instead works with assumptions of slower revenue growth and a sharp compression in margins over the next few years, which are used to derive expected earnings of about £1.7b by 2028.
  • Key concerns in this view include dependence on widebody engines, higher ongoing costs from supply chain and decarbonization pressures, and the drag from pension and debt obligations.
  • On this view, ambitious projects such as UltraFan and SMRs are seen as carrying enough timing and execution risk that a lower Fair Value is judged more appropriate than the current market price.

Taken together, these Narratives present a wide but transparent range for what Rolls-Royce Holdings could be worth. Approach 2: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings can be volatile, the P/S ratio is often a useful way to think about value because it focuses on revenue, which tends to be more stable than profits from year to year.

In general, investors tend to accept a higher “normal” P/S ratio when they expect stronger growth or see lower risk in a company’s cash flows, and a lower ratio when growth expectations are modest or risks appear higher.

Pebblebrook Hotel Trust currently trades on a P/S ratio of 1.07x. That is below the Hotel and Resort REITs industry average P/S of 4.16x and also below the peer average of 1.51x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Pebblebrook Hotel Trust is 1.47x. This is its proprietary view of what an appropriate P/S might be after considering factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk profile.

The Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for the company’s specific characteristics rather than assuming all businesses in the group deserve the same multiple. Since the current 1.07x P/S is below the 1.47x Fair Ratio, the shares appear undervalued on this P/S basis.

Most Popular Narrative: 4,904.2% Overvalued

GLU’s widely followed narrative sets a fair value of $1.90 for Hasbro, far below the last close at $95.08, and frames a very bearish view on the equity story.

Hasbro seems to be doing okay; on the spreadsheets and analyses, it seems they're recovering, it seems there is a plan unfolding. But nothing is father from the truth. These numbers are detached from what happens on the ground; the child buying toys in the store, the fan buying products online; the 'real' things that, when aggregated, account for revenue. And they show a continued decline.
